# CrossFrame Skill

CrossFrame is a Chinese cross-scale structural diagnosis skill. It helps AI analyze complex relationships, teams, organizations, institutions, public disputes, and long-term evolution by first extracting facts, routing to the right protocol, preserving high-risk concepts, then outputting a visible reasoning outline and readable Chinese judgment.

## Canonical

- [Skill entry](skills/crossframe/SKILL.md): canonical Codex skill instructions.
- [Suite skill](skills/crossframe-suite/SKILL.md): route complex multi-skill CrossFrame workflows.
- [Suite routing map](skills/crossframe-suite/references/workflow-routing-map.md): choose continuous skill chains and avoid overtriggering.
- [Essay skill entry](skills/crossframe-essay/SKILL.md): CrossFrame-based Chinese critical insight essay writing skill.
- [Review skill](skills/crossframe-review/SKILL.md): audit CrossFrame outputs for reasoning fidelity, evidence boundaries, citations, and repair steps.
- [Dialogue skill](skills/crossframe-dialogue/SKILL.md): short reader replies, editor responses, and consultation-style structural answers.
- [Casebook skill](skills/crossframe-casebook/SKILL.md): turn materials into reusable CrossFrame casebook entries.
- [Public skill](skills/crossframe-public/SKILL.md): public issues, platform appeals, institutions, policy, and compliance evidence boundaries.
- [Org skill](skills/crossframe-org/SKILL.md): team, project, and organization repair memos and feedback write-back plans.
- [Teach skill](skills/crossframe-teach/SKILL.md): plain-language CrossFrame concept lessons with boundaries, signals, and exercises.
- [Debate skill](skills/crossframe-debate/SKILL.md): proposition testing through both sides, premises, evidence, rebuttals, and withdrawal conditions.
- [Notebook skill](skills/crossframe-notebook/SKILL.md): bidirectional reading notes for books, theories, articles, and excerpts.
- [Concept elevation protocol](skills/crossframe-essay/protocols/concept-elevation-protocol.md): elevate structure judgments into concepts, references, and allusions.
- [Reference and allusion rules](skills/crossframe-essay/references/reference-and-allusion-rules.md): safe quote, paraphrase, allusion, and theory mapping rules.
- [Editorial comrade voice protocol](skills/crossframe-essay/protocols/editorial-comrade-voice-protocol.md): modern editor-style answer voice for patient, humble, serious, decisive Chinese essays.
- [Editorial voice principles](skills/crossframe-essay/references/editorial-voice-principles.md): tone, address, criticism, and closing rules for editor-comrade prose.
- [Routing map](skills/crossframe/references/read-routing-map.md): choose protocols, worksheets, concept cards, and templates.
- [Term fidelity](skills/crossframe/references/v2-term-fidelity.md): preserve v2.0 concept meaning.
- [Coverage map](skills/crossframe/references/v2-coverage-map.md): map v2.0 modules to skill files.
- [Smoke tests](skills/crossframe/evals/crossframe-smoke-tests.md): check that outputs actually reason instead of stacking terms.
- [Essay smoke tests](skills/crossframe-essay/evals/crossframe-essay-smoke-tests.md): check dossier-first article outputs.

## Adapters

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): generic agent and Codex-compatible entry.
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): Claude Code entry.
- [Claude skill](.claude/skills/crossframe/SKILL.md): project skill for Claude Code.
- [Claude suite skill](.claude/skills/crossframe-suite/SKILL.md): project skill for multi-skill workflow routing.
- [Claude essay skill](.claude/skills/crossframe-essay/SKILL.md): project skill for CrossFrame Essay.
- [Cursor rule](.cursor/rules/crossframe.mdc): Cursor rule.
- [Cursor suite rule](.cursor/rules/crossframe-suite.mdc): Cursor rule for multi-skill workflow routing.
- [Cursor essay rule](.cursor/rules/crossframe-essay.mdc): Cursor rule for essay writing.
- [Gemini entry](GEMINI.md): Gemini CLI entry.
- [Copilot instructions](.github/copilot-instructions.md): GitHub Copilot repository instructions.
- [Interfaces](INTERFACES.md): adapter matrix and maintenance order.

## Rule

Do not treat CrossFrame as a concept pile. Always route, fill internal worksheets, preserve high-risk concepts, then answer in plain Chinese.

For complex tasks that need multiple outputs or multiple sibling skills, use CrossFrame Suite first to choose the chain and avoid reading every skill. Treat CrossFrame Suite as the default entry: when the user asks for open-ended analysis without specifying another artifact, route to `crossframe -> crossframe-essay -> crossframe-review` and produce a readable article-style answer. Do not generate an article when the user explicitly asks for review, casebook, memo, table, checklist, action plan, or pure diagnosis. For article tasks, do not write only prose. Use CrossFrame Essay to produce `结构洞察底稿` first, then `文章正文`. When depth is needed, add concept elevation and classic/theory references, but direct quotes must be verifiable and references must return to concrete reality. When the user asks for a warmer answer, use the modern editor-comrade voice: patient and friendly, but still decisive about responsibility and evidence. For specialized workflows, use the matching sibling skill and keep it as a light wrapper over the canonical CrossFrame routing map.
